name: Pat Martino
abstract: Pat Martino (born Patrick Carmen Azzara; August 25, 1944 – November 1, 2021) was an American jazz guitarist and composer.

concerts.label: JVC Jazz Festival: Les Paul 90th Birthday Salute
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

concerts.label: Pat Martino Organ Trio Plus Horns

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: I just bought a record last week by a young guitarist, a young Italian guitarist by the name of Pat Martino. And he's just tremendous, tremendous techniques, good ideas.

about_quotes.speaker: Joanne Brackeen
about_quotes.evidence: there was a Town Hall concert we played that Randy Brecker and Dave Liebman were the horn players and Pat Martino was on guitar.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joanne Brackeen interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 10, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: George Benson
about_quotes.evidence: Willis had just hired this genius, Pat Martino, who was younger than me and played twice as much guitar.
about_quotes.doc_title: George Benson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Eric Alexander
about_quotes.evidence: All the guys that I came up playing with... Pat Martino, same kind of thing.
about_quotes.doc_title: Eric Alexander interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 31, 2010
